title: Shadows on the Atlantic
full_description: Through this permanent exhibition at Het Scheepvaartmuseum, the museum confronts its collection's entanglement with the Dutch slave trade and colonial violence in the Atlantic world. 17th and 18th-century paintings and a two-meter ship model (the D'Keulse Galy, which transported enslaved people from West Africa to the Americas) are presented alongside new works commissioned from artists Atong Atem, Manuwi C Tokai, Robin Hoed, and Wouter Pocornie, whose pieces show how that history still resonates today.
ai_summary: Het Scheepvaartmuseum confronts its own collection — 17th-century paintings and a scale model of a slave ship — in a permanent exhibition about the Dutch role in the Atlantic slave trade, anchored by new commissions from contemporary artists asking what that history means now.

related_events.title: Ekō: Japan in Two Visual Narratives
